title: In Nairobi, Macron ends a decade of turmoil in France-Africa relations
subhed: Coming to power having promised to rebuild the relationship between France and its former colonies, Emmanuel Macron had to face a series of crises, particularly in the Sahel region. In the English-speaking part of the continent, Paris struggled to find its place.

title: Hantavirus: After first positive case in France, government aims to 'break chain' of transmission

Embattled UK PM Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong
subhed: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who has been mired in the scandal around Jeffrey Epstein and criticism over his policies, sought to react to Thursday's local elections, in which his Labour party lost nearly 1,500 council seats.

title: In Madagascar, Russia seeks a new African stronghold
subhed: After bursting onto the scene alongside the junta, which seized power in October 2025, by providing it with weapons and military instructors from Africa Corps, Moscow has been advancing its pawns on many fronts.

title: Spanish woman priest challenges the Vatican
subhed: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.

title: Who is Iyad Ag Ghali, the jihadist behind the destabilization of Mali?
subhed: A central figure in Sahelian jihad for nearly two decades, pursued in turn by the French and the Russians, Ghali led the offensive against the junta in Bamako. Since 2012, the jihadist and leader of Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has softened his rhetoric, seeking to appear more approachable and less radical.

The social measures tech leaders put forward to address AI's destructive potential sound familiar in Europe
subhed: Aware of the potentially devastating impact their technology could have on jobs and society, the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are advancing a range of progressive ideas aimed at preserving social cohesion.

title: Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
subhed: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

title: How chicken is taking over France's fast-food market
subhed: The number of fast-food chains dedicated to chicken is booming in France. At the heart of this success is affordable comfort food, offering an unbeatable quantity-for-price ratio at a time when purchasing power is on the decline.
